keyRules: Up to 120 m above the ground (EASA Open category)
Registration as a UAS operator (€40 / 30 months) for drones weighing 250 g or more or equipped with a camera
Free A1/A3 online exam available through CAA Slovenia

Prohibited over populated areas—the only exception is for drones weighing up to 1,200 g, flying at altitudes of up to 50 m, with the landowner’s consent and prior notification of the flight to the agency.
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
At least 5 km from any airport or airfield
Night flying is prohibited without a special permit
Triglav National Park: Flying drones is prohibited without prior permission from the park administration.
CTR (e.g., Ljubljana): more than 5 km from the airport, fly at a maximum altitude of 50 m above ground level; closer or higher only with the approval of Slovenian Air Traffic Control.
Nuclear Facility Area (NEK Krško): Flying is permitted only with authorization from the Slovenian Nuclear Safety Administration and the Ministry of the Interior.
importantNotes: The online training and the A1/A3 exam are free (included in the registration).
Flying over populated areas requires additional permits
The Triglav National Park has restrictions on drones
The GDPR applies to the collection of personally identifiable information

fields.disclaimerText: Always check official CAA Slovenia sources before flying. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Slovenia follows EASA regulations with national amendments.
